name: Switch From Postpaid to Prepaid Mid-Cycle Without Losing Balance
description: Call a mobile carrier to convert a line from postpaid to prepaid (same carrier or to their prepaid sub-brand) mid-billing-cycle — keeping the number, salvaging any unused prepaid balance/credit, and avoiding double-billing for the cycle.
disclaimer: Switching from postpaid to prepaid on the SAME carrier is often a port-internal flow that loses none of the number's history. Switching to a DIFFERENT carrier's prepaid brand is a number port — use `port-mobile-number-survive-retention` for the port-out side. Device installment plans on postpaid typically accelerate to a final balance on conversion; confirm before pulling the trigger.

context.when_to_use: User wants to lower mobile costs by moving to prepaid (same carrier's prepaid brand like Cricket / Visible / Metro / Mint) and is in the middle of their postpaid billing cycle. Best near the END of the postpaid cycle so the user isn't paying postpaid for days they won't use.
preconditions: User has identified the target prepaid plan and price.
Account number, account holder name, billing zip on postpaid line.
Knowledge of any device-installment balance still owed.
Whether the user has any unused postpaid credit (autopay credit, referral credit, manufacturer rebate) — these often DO NOT transfer to prepaid.
Current postpaid bill date and next-cycle start date.

principles: Time the switch to the LAST 1-3 days of the postpaid cycle. Postpaid is paid in advance for service through end-of-cycle — switching mid-cycle leaves money on the table.
Burn any non-transferable postpaid credits BEFORE the switch (international roaming credit, device-protection credit, etc.). Once converted, those credits typically zero out.
Some carriers run 'switch and save' incentives between their postpaid and prepaid arms — ask explicitly. Reps don't always volunteer the cross-brand incentive.
If you're routed to retention because the rep heard 'I want prepaid', recognise it within 60 seconds. Retention will try to keep you on postpaid; you want sales / activations for the prepaid brand.
Confirm the eSIM / SIM logistics BEFORE confirming the conversion. Some prepaid brands require a new SIM ship; the gap between activation and SIM arrival is a day or two of no service.

phrases.opener: Hi, I want to convert line [number] from postpaid to prepaid — moving to the [target plan name] on [prepaid brand]. My postpaid cycle ends [date]. I want to make sure timing is right and I'm not paying twice.
phrases.queue_check: Are you the team that handles postpaid-to-prepaid conversions, or is that a different group? I want to be with whoever can actually flip the switch.
phrases.transfer_request: Could you do a warm transfer to the prepaid activations / conversions team and stay on the line until they pick up? I'd rather not lose my hold position.
phrases.decline_retention_polite: Thanks for the offer — I've already evaluated the postpaid plans. I'm moving to prepaid to lock in the price. I'd rather not spend time on a save offer today.
phrases.ask_cycle_alignment: When exactly should the conversion happen so I don't pay overlap? My postpaid cycle ends [date] — can we schedule activation on [date - 1 or date]?
phrases.device_installment_check: I have a device on installments — balance about $[X]. What happens to that balance on conversion? Does it accelerate, get split, or follow me to prepaid?
phrases.credits_check: Do I have any non-transferable credits on this account — autopay credit, manufacturer rebate, account credit? I want to burn them before the conversion if they won't carry.
phrases.sim_logistics: Will I need a new SIM or eSIM for the prepaid line? If physical, when does it ship and can I activate before it arrives? I can't have a service gap.
phrases.ask_switch_promo: Is there a 'switch and save' bonus running between [postpaid brand] and [prepaid brand] — first-month free, bill credit, port-in bonus? Reps don't always volunteer it.
phrases.lock_in_writing: Please email confirmation of: conversion date, final postpaid bill amount, device installment status, SIM logistics, and confirmation that the number is preserved.
phrases.graceful_close: Thanks. Conversion on [date], device balance $[X] handled by [method], SIM via [eSIM/ship], confirmation number [number], number preserved.

tactics.name: Align the switch with cycle end
tactics.script: Use `ask_cycle_alignment`. Conversion mid-cycle = paying postpaid for unused days. The 1-3 days before cycle end is the sweet spot.

tactics.name: Recognise wrong-queue / retention
tactics.when: Rep starts running postpaid save offers.
tactics.script: Use `queue_check` and `transfer_request`. The prepaid activation / conversion team is the one that can actually perform the conversion. Retention's job is to prevent it.

tactics.name: Settle the device installment question first
tactics.script: Use `device_installment_check`. If the balance accelerates to a final bill, the user may want to pay it down first or delay conversion. If it 'follows' to a sub-brand, less of an issue.

tactics.name: Burn non-transferable credits before converting
tactics.script: Use `credits_check`. Autopay credits, port-in promos, manufacturer rebates frequently zero out on postpaid-to-prepaid conversion. Use them up first (or apply them to the final bill).

tactics.name: Ask explicitly about switch incentives
tactics.script: Use `ask_switch_promo`. Same-carrier-family conversions often have hidden incentives — first month free on prepaid, port-in bonus, bill credit. Not volunteered unless asked.

tactics.name: Pin SIM / eSIM logistics
tactics.script: Use `sim_logistics`. Activating before the SIM arrives leaves a service gap. eSIM avoids this entirely if the device supports it.

tactics.name: Get it in writing
tactics.script: Use `lock_in_writing`. Conversion bugs (number lost, double-bill, device balance disappearing into the void) are common — written confirmation is the lever to fix them.

boundaries: Do NOT cancel the postpaid line as a separate step before converting — that releases the number and can break the conversion.

Do NOT convert if a critical device-installment promo (e.g. 'pay $0 for the iPhone with 36 months of postpaid service') is still running. Conversion typically voids the promo and accelerates the full retail price.
Do NOT skip checking whether the prepaid plan has the same data / hotspot / international coverage. Cheaper plans often cut these silently.
Do NOT trust autopay-credit promises that 'transfer over' — get the dollar amount and apply mechanism in writing.
success_signals: Conversion scheduled at end of postpaid cycle.
Device installment status disclosed with clear next step.
Non-transferable credits identified and burn plan in place.
SIM/eSIM logistics confirmed with no service gap.
failure_signals: Rep insists conversion must take effect today (will create overlap charge).
Device balance status is hand-waved — no clear final amount.
Switch is being processed as a 'cancel + new account' instead of a conversion — that's a port-out, treat it as such.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm date, balances, SIM logistics. Calendar the conversion date and a 7-day follow-up to verify billing landed correctly.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Step back and treat as a full port-out to a different carrier — sometimes a competitor prepaid (Mint / US Mobile / Tello) is cleaner than the same-family prepaid arm. Use `port-mobile-number-survive-retention`.
follow_ups: Day before scheduled conversion: confirm via app the work order is still set.
Day after conversion: verify the line is active on prepaid, the number is preserved, and that postpaid shows final-bill status.
Within 14 days: verify the final postpaid bill matches expectations and no recurring postpaid charge continues.
Within 30 days: verify all credits/incentives applied as promised.

required_user_info: Account number, holder name, billing zip on postpaid
Device installment balance (if any) and device finance terms
